articles.title: What’s the best month to list your house in Greater Boston?
articles.description:
Boston-area trends may contradict national data

The post What’s the best month to list your house in Greater Boston? appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Listing homes in Greater Boston peaks in April, contrary to national trends.
April listings increased by 15% compared to March 2023, boosting local market activity.
Homeowners can maximize sale prices by listing in spring, benefiting Boston's real estate market.

articles.title: ‘Boston College is about to have a renaissance in basketball’: Dan Hurley’s take on Luke Murray
articles.description:
The UConn coach had strong praise for his outgoing assistant, who is now set to take over Eagles basketball.

The post ‘Boston College is about to have a renaissance in basketball’: Dan Hurley’s take on Luke Murray appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: UConn coach Dan Hurley praises Luke Murray as Boston College's new basketball head coach.
Murray's appointment signals a potential revival for Boston College basketball programs.
Local fans anticipate increased competitiveness in NCAA basketball starting next season.

articles.title: MBTA sets $80 round-trip train fare for World Cup games
articles.description:
Sold exclusively on mTicket, tickets will be available Wednesday, April 8.

The post MBTA sets $80 round-trip train fare for World Cup games appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: MBTA announces $80 round-trip train fare for World Cup games in 2026.
Tickets go on sale April 8, exclusively via mTicket app.
Boston residents can easily access games without driving or parking hassles.

articles.title: New on Netflix and more: Top streaming picks for April 2026
articles.description:
From a new Netflix zombie thriller to classics like “The Truman Show” and “The Devil Wears Prada,” here’s what to stream this month.

The post New on Netflix and more: Top streaming picks for April 2026 appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Netflix releases a new zombie thriller for Boston residents to enjoy in April 2026.
Classic films like 'The Truman Show' and 'The Devil Wears Prada' are also available this month.
Streaming options increase for local viewers, enhancing entertainment choices during spring.

articles.title: The double lives of real estate agents: Why many Massachusetts brokers have day jobs
articles.description:
Meg Grady starts her day before 4:30 am. She immediately responds to emails, reviews tenant applications, then joins calls with India and works a full day as a remote strategic sourcing associate for a financial consulting firm. Come 5 p.m., she starts her second full-time job as a real estate agent, running showings until about […]

The post The double lives of real estate agents: Why many Massachusetts brokers have day jobs appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Many Massachusetts real estate agents, like Meg Grady, juggle day jobs and real estate careers.
Grady works as a remote associate during the day and shows properties after 5 p.m.
This trend reflects the financial pressures on agents in Boston's competitive housing market.

articles.title: Details emerge on fatal police shooting of 27-year-old woman in N.H.
articles.description:
Megan Whiting was "armed with a knife within close proximity of an injured female resident" when an officer fired, authorities say.

The post Details emerge on fatal police shooting of 27-year-old woman in N.H. appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Megan Whiting, 27, was shot by police while armed with a knife on April 7.
Incident occurred near an injured female resident, raising safety concerns in the community.
The shooting may lead to increased scrutiny of police protocols in New Hampshire.

articles.title: Signature Healthcare in Brockton hit by cybersecurity incident
articles.description:
The hospital temporarily shut down some services, but inpatient and walk-in emergency services remain open.

The post Signature Healthcare in Brockton hit by cybersecurity incident appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Signature Healthcare in Brockton experienced a cybersecurity incident affecting some services.
Inpatient and walk-in emergency services remain operational despite the incident.
Local residents may face delays in non-emergency services during the shutdown.
